name: crestron-mcp
description: Query and control a Crestron 4-Series AV system in natural language.
long_description: Connects Claude to a Crestron 4-Series processor running the CrestronMCP modules. Lets you discover rooms and devices, read and set lighting, AV, HVAC and shades, and smoothly ramp analog levels - all in plain English. Talks to the processor over the CrestronMCP text protocol, with optional password (mode 1) or secure-key + TLS (mode 2) authentication.

tools.name: discover_crestron_system
tools.description: Discover rooms, categories, and device counts in the Crestron system.

tools.name: list_crestron_rooms
tools.description: List all rooms with their device counts.

tools.name: list_crestron_devices
tools.description: List controllable devices, optionally filtered by room and/or category.

tools.name: query_crestron_device
tools.description: Get the live state of a device: value plus whether it is idle, ramping (target + completes_at), or pulsing (releases_at), and any pending scheduled action.

tools.name: get_crestron_time
tools.description: Get the processor's current time as epoch milliseconds and ISO 8601.

tools.name: control_crestron_device
tools.description: Set a device's value (digital, analog, or serial), optionally after a delay.

tools.name: set_crestron_devices
tools.description: Set multiple devices at once in one round trip - for scenes/macros.

tools.name: pulse_crestron_device
tools.description: Momentarily pulse a digital device on then off (a simulated button press), optionally after a delay.

tools.name: ramp_crestron_device
tools.description: Smoothly ramp (fade) an analog device to a value over a duration.

tools.name: cancel_crestron_device
tools.description: Stop a ramp/pulse in progress and clear any pending delayed action on a device, without otherwise changing its value.

tools.name: get_room_status
tools.description: Get the status of every device in a room.

tools.name: activate_crestron_license
tools.description: Activate (license) the processor with a key the user provides, when a command reports the processor is unlicensed. One-time; stored on the processor.

tools.name: get_crestron_license_status
tools.description: Check the processor's license/trial state: licensed now, whether it's a time-limited trial, remaining trial time, MAC, and a buy URL.

tools.name: start_crestron_trial
tools.description: Start a free 7-day trial on the processor (no payment). Up to 3 per processor; reports how many remain, or a buy link when used up.

host.title: Processor address
host.description: IP address or hostname of the Crestron processor on your network (e.g. 10.0.1.38).

port.title: Port
port.description: TCP port the CrestronMCP server listens on.

key.title: Secure key
key.description: Recommended. The processor's secure key, shown on the MCP Server Config module's Key output. Setting it turns on encryption (TLS) and secure authentication automatically. Leave blank only if your processor is in open or password mode.

password.title: Password (only for password mode)
password.description: Use this only if your processor is set to password mode. Leave blank otherwise. Ignored when a secure key is set.
